meaning: only when / of all things, specifically (ironic or frustrated emphasis)

exercises.example_sentence: 大切なときに限って、電話が繋がらない。
exercises.translation: Of all times, my phone will not connect when it really matters.
wrong_choice_explanations: This pattern does not convey the same meaning as '〜に限って' (only when / of all things, specifically (ironic or frustrated emphasis)) needed in this context.
This pattern does not convey the same meaning as '〜に限って' (only when / of all things, specifically (ironic or frustrated emphasis)) needed in this context.
This pattern does not convey the same meaning as '〜に限って' (only when / of all things, specifically (ironic or frustrated emphasis)) needed in this context.

exercises.example_sentence: ほしい商品に限って、品切れになっている。
exercises.translation: Of all things, the item I want is out of stock.
wrong_choice_explanations: にだけ has a different grammatical function and doesn't fit the context of this sentence where に限って is required.
に限り has a different grammatical function and doesn't fit the context of this sentence where に限って is required.
だけに has a different grammatical function and doesn't fit the context of this sentence where に限って is required.

exercises.example_sentence: 重要な会議に限って、遅刻をしてしまう。
exercises.translation: Of all meetings, I tend to be late to important ones.
wrong_choice_explanations: にだけ has a different grammatical function, but 〜に限って is needed to express the specific meaning required in this sentence.
に限り means only / as long as, but 〜に限って has a specific meaning, which is what this sentence requires.
だけに has a different grammatical function, but 〜に限って is needed to express the specific meaning required in this sentence.

exercises.example_sentence: 絶対に失敗したくない時に限って、ミスをする。
exercises.translation: Of all times, I make mistakes when I really don't want to.
wrong_choice_explanations: にだけ has a different grammatical function that doesn't fit this sentence context.
に限り has a different grammatical function that doesn't fit this sentence context.
だけに has a different grammatical function that doesn't fit this sentence context.

exercises.example_sentence: 大事な試験に限って、風邪をひいてしまった。
exercises.translation: Of all times, I caught a cold during an important exam.
wrong_choice_explanations: 〜にだけ is simple limitation; lacks ironic emphasis.
〜に限り is more formal; different from frustrated tone.
〜だけに emphasizes cause; doesn't convey ironic timing.
